titleEn: If food security is strategic, why does agricultural innovation still struggle to scale?
titleZh: 粮食安全是战略问题，为何农业创新仍难规模化？
summaryZh: 本文探讨了农业技术创新规模化面临的困境。文章指出，农业长期受益于其他行业（如GPS、传感器、AI）的技术溢出，但如今农业自身正成为新技术的试验场，这些技术最终却在建筑、采矿甚至国防领域找到更大市场。文章以Monarch Tractor为例，暗示农业创新在资本、产业链适配和规模化路径上存在结构性障碍，值得智慧畜牧从业者反思技术落地的现实挑战。
featuredReason: 本文从技术溢出与产业落地的逆向视角，揭示了农业创新规模化受阻的深层原因，对智慧畜牧行业思考技术投资与商业化路径具有启发意义。
contentFull: If food security is strategic, why does agricultural innovation still struggle to scale? 15-06 | Tech in focus | Expert opinionLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrint Geert Hekkert Chief editor of Future FarmingPhoto: AI-generatedFor decades, agriculture has benefited from technologies developed elsewhere. GPS came from defense. Sensors and cameras originated in the automotive industry. Artificial intelligence emerged from Silicon Valley.But something interesting is happening. Increasingly, agriculture itself is becoming the proving ground for technologies that eventually find their largest markets elsewhere—in construction, mining and, increasingly, defense.No longer a theoretical discussionTake Monarch Tractor. AfterLinkedInFacebookXWhatsAppPrintMore aboutagtech investmentsartificial intelligenceautonomous technologyelectric tractorssustainabilityadLEARN MORERelated articles02-07 | NewsTersus GNSS AG993: Modular autosteer kit with built-in satellite correction fallbackPremium30-06 | Photo reportDLG Feldtage: hot, dusty and relatively quiet25-06 | NewsAgreenculture acquires Chouette to add agronomic intelligence to autonomous machines25-06 | NewsFieldwork Robotics secures new funding to accelerate raspberry harvesting robot Buyers guides & Catalogues Subscribe to our YouTube channel Taiwan Smart Agriweek 2026Sep 8-10, 2026 TaiNEX 1, Tapei
translationZh:
几十年来，农业一直受益于其他领域开发的技术。GPS来自国防领域。传感器和摄像头源于汽车工业。人工智能则出自硅谷。

但有趣的事情正在发生。农业本身正日益成为新技术的试验场，而这些技术最终却在建筑、采矿以及日益增长的国防领域找到了最大的市场。

**不再只是理论探讨**
以Monarch Tractor为例。
author: Geert Hekkert
contentHtml: For decades, agriculture has benefited from technologies developed elsewhere. GPS came from defense. Sensors and cameras originated in the automotive industry. Artificial intelligence emerged from Silicon Valley.
